title: The One with Frank, Jr.

jokes.text: So, what happened? Did a forest tick you off?
jokes.setup: Seeing someone covered in sawdust/wood debris
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'tick off' (anger) vs forest ticks (insects)
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay with good economy. The pun works on multiple levels but isn't groundbreaking.

jokes.text: You're building a post office?
jokes.setup: Ross mentions needing a place for mail
jokes.punchline: Monica jumps to the absurd conclusion of building an entire post office
jokes.explanation: Good escalation humor. Monica's tendency to overthink creates the absurd leap.

jokes.text: My word, those are snug!
jokes.setup: Ross wearing tight old work pants
jokes.punchline: Monica's overly polite way of saying his pants are way too tight
jokes.explanation: Monica's prissy vocabulary contrasts with the obvious visual comedy of Ross's tight pants.

jokes.text: Sergio Valente
jokes.setup: Explaining his tight old work pants
jokes.punchline: Brand name reveals these are dated designer jeans from the 80s
jokes.explanation: Perfect brand reference for 90s audience. Ross's attempt to sound fashionable with outdated brand.

jokes.text: Power saw kind of got away from me there
jokes.setup: Ross covered in sawdust and debris
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement of obvious construction disaster
jokes.explanation: Ross's academic tendency to understate creates good contrast with visual chaos.

jokes.text: I think it sounds big sister-y, 'Frank's always late.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe saying Frank is always late despite meeting him once
jokes.punchline: Self-aware joke about her pretending to know him well
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment. Phoebe's desire to feel like a big sister despite lack of relationship.

jokes.text: Mom's dead, don't talk to my sister... Grandma's been sleeping a lot lately
jokes.setup: Explaining why meeting Frank is important
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic way of saying Grandma is probably dying too
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe dark humor delivered matter-of-factly. The euphemism makes it funnier.

jokes.text: It's just the last-desperate-chance-to-have-a-family kind of thing
jokes.setup: Serious family situation
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's casual delivery of deeply sad reality
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's ability to make tragic situations sound casual and funny through delivery.

jokes.text: Actually, Gunther sent me. You're not allowed to have cups out here
jokes.setup: Monica seeming sweet for waiting with Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's just enforcing coffee shop rules
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of expectation. Monica's rule-following nature creates the punchline.

jokes.text: No. But he told me he thinks you're a fox
jokes.setup: Monica asking if anyone thinks David Copperfield is cute
jokes.punchline: Rachel pretends Copperfield commented on Monica's looks
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection joke. Works but not particularly clever or character-specific.

jokes.text: The heart of every healthy relationship: Honesty, respect and sex with celebrities
jokes.setup: Chandler explaining the celebrity freebie list concept
jokes.punchline: Monica's sarcastic summary of relationship priorities
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure with the rule of three, perfect sarcastic timing, highly quotable.

jokes.text: You do realize that she is a cartoon and way out of your league?
jokes.setup: Chandler listing Jessica Rabbit on his celebrity list
jokes.punchline: Double burn - she's not real AND he couldn't get her anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect double insult. The escalation from 'cartoon' to 'out of your league' is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: I know. I always wondered if I could get her eyes to pop out of her head
jokes.setup: Being called out for choosing cartoon Jessica Rabbit
jokes.punchline: Chandler doubles down with reference to her cartoon physics
jokes.explanation: Chandler's inability to back down from bad choices creates good character comedy.

jokes.text: And also, of course, if her sister was famous
jokes.setup: Explaining how the list could have saved his relationship with Annie
jokes.punchline: Absurd additional requirement that her sister be famous
jokes.explanation: Chandler's tendency to add unnecessary complications to simple concepts.

jokes.text: Spider-Man? / Hardy Boy. / Peter Parker. / Thank you.
jokes.setup: Rachel mentions Parker Stevenson on her list
jokes.punchline: Confusion between Parker Stevenson (Hardy Boys) and Peter Parker (Spider-Man)
jokes.explanation: Simple name confusion gag. Works for the moment but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: First, I'd divide my prospective candidates into categories
jokes.setup: Being asked who would be on his celebrity list
jokes.punchline: Ross's over-analytical approach to what should be fun
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross character moment. His academic nature applied to ridiculous situation.

jokes.text: What a geek!
jokes.setup: Ross's over-analytical response about celebrity lists
jokes.punchline: Monica's blunt reaction to her brother's nerdiness
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling dynamic. Monica's directness contrasts with Ross's pretension.

jokes.text: Frank stares at Rachel in stunned silence
jokes.setup: Frank meeting all of Phoebe's attractive friends
jokes.punchline: Frank is clearly overwhelmed by Rachel's beauty
jokes.explanation: Standard visual gag about Rachel's attractiveness. Predictable but functional.

jokes.text: Hey, how do you guys get anything done? / We don't, really.
jokes.setup: Frank asking how they accomplish things with all the attractive people
jokes.punchline: Joey's honest admission that they don't
jokes.explanation: Good setup-punchline with Joey's characteristic honesty about their laziness.

jokes.text: I thought we could go down to Times Square and pick up some ninja stars
jokes.setup: Planning activities with his half-sister
jokes.punchline: Frank's idea of sibling bonding involves weapons
jokes.explanation: Establishes Frank's character as somewhat dangerous/immature in a funny way.

jokes.text: My friend Larry, he wants me to take a picture of a hooker
jokes.setup: Continuing to describe his touristy plans
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning inappropriate tourist activity
jokes.explanation: Frank's casual mention of something inappropriate creates good cringe comedy.

jokes.text: We really don't take advantage of living in the city
jokes.setup: Frank's inappropriate tourist plans
jokes.punchline: Monica's ironic comment about their failure to be tourists
jokes.explanation: Good ironic commentary on the contrast between Frank's plans and their lifestyle.

jokes.text: Sorry. Did I get you? / No, you didn't get me! It's an electric drill! You get me, you kill me!
jokes.setup: Joey using power tools near Chandler
jokes.punchline: Chandler's panicked overreaction to near-miss with drill
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from casual question to life-or-death panic. Chandler's dramatic delivery sells it.

jokes.text: I'm not a mind reader
jokes.setup: Chandler saying he doesn't want the entertainment unit
jokes.punchline: Joey's obliviousness to the obvious signals
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey obliviousness. The irony is that he clearly SHOULD be able to read these signals.

jokes.text: Where you heading in those pants? 1982?
jokes.setup: Seeing Ross in his tight vintage pants
jokes.punchline: Joey's time-travel insult about outdated fashion
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the pants established earlier. Joey's delivery style makes it quotable.

jokes.text: If you move your hamper... you can see the color the tile used to be
jokes.setup: Joey trying to convince Monica her floor is dirty
jokes.punchline: Revealing the floor is so dirty there's clean areas under furniture
jokes.explanation: Good reveal that works both visually and as setup for Monica's cleaning obsession trigger.

jokes.text: A little more than I wanted to see
jokes.setup: Joey demonstrating how easily the tile comes up
jokes.punchline: Monica's reaction to seeing the full extent of her floor's dirtiness
jokes.explanation: Monica's horror at mess is perfectly in character. The understated reaction works.

jokes.text: We can put it back there after the surgeons remove it from your colon
jokes.setup: Joey suggesting where to put a tool
jokes.punchline: Monica's violent threat disguised as helpful suggestion
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke. Monica's cleaning obsession drives her to creative violent imagery.

jokes.text: Triple
jokes.setup: Joey saying Chandler pays him nothing for the work
jokes.punchline: Monica offering triple nothing (still nothing) in her desperation
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one word that reveals Monica's desperation and poor math skills.

jokes.text: Because I can and my mom couldn't. And I figured that was something I got from our dad
jokes.setup: Frank asking Phoebe if she can roll her tongue
jokes.punchline: Frank's innocent but awkward way of discussing genetics and family
jokes.explanation: Frank's earnest but clumsy attempt to find connection creates gentle cringe comedy.

jokes.text: We don't have that
jokes.setup: Frank and Phoebe discovering they can't both roll their tongues
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's matter-of-fact dismissal of their genetic connection
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's casual dismissal of their failed bonding attempt is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: That's close
jokes.setup: Learning someone's birthday is February 18th vs his 16th
jokes.punchline: Frank thinking two days apart is 'close' for birthdays
jokes.explanation: Frank's desperate attempt to find connections leads to silly logic.

jokes.text: That's the same month as Halloween
jokes.setup: Phoebe's birthday is October 25th
jokes.punchline: Frank's attempt to find connection through Halloween being in October
jokes.explanation: Frank's continued desperate search for commonality creates good character comedy.

jokes.text: Melt stuff
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking what he likes to do at home
jokes.punchline: Frank's simple, literal answer about his destructive hobby
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character establishment. Two words that define Frank's character.

jokes.text: Forgiving
jokes.setup: Chandler saying Elizabeth Hurley is attractive
jokes.punchline: Ross's knowing reference to her staying with Hugh Grant after scandal
jokes.explanation: Perfect topical reference for 90s audience. One word captures entire tabloid scandal.

jokes.text: She probably wouldn't let you do it unless you donated four cans of food first
jokes.setup: Ross saying Susan Sarandon is too political
jokes.punchline: Chandler's specific joke about her activism affecting bedroom activities
jokes.explanation: Specific, absurd image that perfectly captures Susan Sarandon's activist reputation.

jokes.text: Because that's why you won't get Isabella Rossellini. Geography.
jokes.setup: Ross giving practical advice about celebrity list choices
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic point about Ross's delusions of celebrity access
jokes.explanation: Excellent sarcasm that punctures Ross's pretentious analysis. Great setup for later payoff.

jokes.text: So by 'melting' you meant melting
jokes.setup: Discovering Frank literally melts objects as a hobby
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's realization that there's no metaphor involved
jokes.explanation: Good callback that emphasizes how literal and simple Frank is.

jokes.text: Yeah, you can melt art
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking if melting is like art
jokes.punchline: Frank's matter-of-fact response about melting artwork
jokes.explanation: Frank's literal interpretation creates absurdist humor about destroying art.

jokes.text: No, I want to melt it
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking if he wants to use the phone to call his mom
jokes.punchline: Frank wants to literally melt the phone
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that reinforces Frank's one-track mind about melting things.

jokes.text: Just the numbers. It will still work. Well, not right now.
jokes.setup: Explaining his plan to melt the phone
jokes.punchline: Frank's illogical explanation followed by contradiction
jokes.explanation: Frank's flawed logic and immediate self-contradiction creates good character comedy.

jokes.text: That's when the waistband goes over your head
jokes.setup: Explaining different types of wedgies to Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Monica's technical definition of 'atomic wedgie'
jokes.explanation: Monica's need to provide technical explanations even for childish pranks.

jokes.text: How do you play the shadow game? / I just asked you. / I just asked you.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking about the shadow game
jokes.punchline: Monica demonstrates by mimicking Phoebe's words
jokes.explanation: Simple demonstration of annoying childhood game. Works through repetition.

jokes.text: You just gave up really quickly
jokes.setup: Phoebe getting frustrated with the shadow game
jokes.punchline: Monica's observation about Phoebe's low tolerance for annoyance
jokes.explanation: Good character observation about Phoebe's personality contrasting with siblings' endurance.

jokes.text: It's my fault, really, because the couch is where we keep the varnish
jokes.setup: Joey somehow got stuck to Monica's couch
jokes.punchline: Monica's sarcastic blame of herself for keeping varnish on furniture
jokes.explanation: Monica's sarcasm about the obvious safety hazard Joey created.

jokes.text: Build the unit, Cinderellie Lay the tile, Cinderellie
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler both demanding Joey's help
jokes.punchline: Monica's Cinderella reference to Joey as overworked servant
jokes.explanation: Creative reference that captures Joey's position between demanding friends.

jokes.text: No, I need a bathroom. I'm a girl!
jokes.setup: Fighting with Chandler over Joey's priorities
jokes.punchline: Monica's desperate appeal to gender-based bathroom needs
jokes.explanation: Monica's competitive nature drives her to use any argument to win.

jokes.text: Joey, if this takes seven days... you'll be able to meet God and ask him about it yourself
jokes.setup: Joey saying even God rested on the seventh day
jokes.punchline: Monica's death threat disguised as religious reference
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that turns Joey's religious reference into death threat. Great structure.

jokes.text: It's one of my regulars. He's insisting that I do him
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining why she has to go to work
jokes.punchline: Phrasing that sounds sexual but refers to massage work
jokes.explanation: Standard double entendre that sets up Frank's misconception about Phoebe's job.

jokes.text: Well, you know, we don't call it that. But, yeah.
jokes.setup: Frank asking if she works at a massage parlor
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's response that accidentally confirms his suspicion
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's innocent response accidentally confirms the worst interpretation.

jokes.text: What are big sisters for?
jokes.setup: Offering Frank a free massage at her work
jokes.punchline: Innocent sisterly gesture that sounds inappropriate given context
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's innocent desire to be a good sister creates awkward situation.

jokes.text: Hey, do Monica and Rachel work there?
jokes.setup: Frank excited about going to Phoebe's 'massage parlor'
jokes.punchline: Frank hoping Phoebe's attractive friends also work there
jokes.explanation: Frank's inappropriate question reveals his misunderstanding of Phoebe's job.

jokes.text: Dorothy Hamill
jokes.setup: Reading Ross's final celebrity list
jokes.punchline: Olympic figure skater from the 70s among modern celebrities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross choice - academic, nostalgic, and completely out of touch with current culture.

jokes.text: You do realize she only spins like that on ice?
jokes.setup: Ross choosing Dorothy Hamill for his celebrity list
jokes.punchline: Rachel pointing out the figure skater's abilities are ice-specific
jokes.explanation: Great callback structure to earlier Jessica Rabbit joke format. Good observational humor.

jokes.text: She broke my arm! / He touched my fanny. / She touched mine first! / That's my job!
jokes.setup: Frank and Phoebe's misunderstanding at the massage parlor
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire exchange revealing the inappropriate touching situation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure that reveals the massage parlor misunderstanding through conflict.

jokes.text: No, okay. I know. You're a masseuse. It's cool. I'm not a cop!
jokes.setup: Frank realizing he misunderstood Phoebe's job
jokes.punchline: Frank's 'reassurance' that makes it sound like illegal activity
jokes.explanation: Frank's attempt to be understanding makes him sound more suspicious and clueless.

jokes.text: How I like to melt stuff, and how I dislike stuff that doesn't melt
jokes.setup: Frank explaining their great conversations to Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Frank's binary worldview based entirely on meltability
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy that reveals Frank's simple, binary way of seeing the world.

jokes.text: You hopped a little bit
jokes.setup: Phoebe saying she wasn't 'hopping mad'
jokes.punchline: Frank's literal observation about her actual hopping
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that shows Frank's literal interpretation of idioms.

jokes.text: When I tell my friends about her, she will be
jokes.setup: Phoebe correcting that the woman isn't a hooker
jokes.punchline: Frank admitting he'll lie to make the story more exciting
jokes.explanation: Frank's honest admission about embellishing stories shows his character's simplicity.

jokes.text: Until Frank melted it
jokes.setup: Showing Frank Jr's gift of their dad's garage door opener
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that Frank destroyed the meaningful object
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows Frank's destructive nature ruined their only family heirloom.

jokes.text: I could've counted to three four times without all the two talk
jokes.setup: Debate about counting to two vs three when lifting
jokes.punchline: Joey's frustrated math about wasted time
jokes.explanation: Joey's practical frustration with overthinking creates good character moment.

jokes.text: Maybe all the rulers are wrong
jokes.setup: Entertainment unit being too big for the room
jokes.punchline: Joey's suggestion that measurement tools might be universally wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - rather than admit error, question the fundamental tools of measurement.

jokes.text: Arm hair stuck! Arm hair stuck!
jokes.setup: Ross touching the wet side of the entertainment unit
jokes.punchline: Ross's panicked reaction to being stuck to his own furniture
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy. Ross's panicked delivery sells the moment.

jokes.text: You freak
jokes.setup: Customer ordering extremely complicated coffee drink
jokes.punchline: Gunther's blunt assessment of the ridiculous order
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gunther moment - his deadpan hostility toward annoying customers.

jokes.text: Yeah? Well, missy, you better be glad that list is laminated
jokes.setup: Rachel challenging Ross about approaching Isabella Rossellini
jokes.punchline: Ross's threat to revise his celebrity list as retaliation
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to lamination while showing Ross's petty competitiveness. Great build-up.

jokes.text: You know what, honey? You go ahead. We'll call her an alternate
jokes.setup: Ross threatening to change his list
jokes.punchline: Rachel's competitive response to let him try with Isabella as backup plan
jokes.explanation: Rachel's competitive nature matches Ross's, escalating their relationship dynamic.

jokes.text: So I bumped you for Winona Ryder. Local
jokes.setup: Ross explaining to Isabella why she's not on his final list
jokes.punchline: Revealing he chose based on Chandler's geography advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to earlier setup. Ross's logical approach backfires spectacularly.

jokes.text: You know, it's ironic because I have a list... of five goofy coffeehouse guys. And yesterday, I just bumped you for that guy over there
jokes.setup: Ross explaining his celebrity list concept to Isabella
jokes.punchline: Isabella turns the tables with her own list, removing Ross
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal that deflates Ross completely. Perfect structure and escalation.

jokes.text: I got 5 bucks says you can't
jokes.setup: Joey claiming he can fit in the entertainment unit
jokes.punchline: Chandler's willingness to bet money on Joey's poor judgment
jokes.explanation: Chandler's confidence in Joey's inability to fit sets up the final physical gag.

jokes.text: Well, hello, Mr. Lincoln! Better luck next time, buddy! And the drinks are on me!
jokes.setup: Joey winning the bet by fitting in the entertainment unit
jokes.punchline: Joey's over-the-top celebration while stuck in furniture
jokes.explanation: Joey's victory celebration while trapped creates good physical comedy ending.

tmdb_overview: Phoebe's half-brother visits, but they have trouble bonding. Joey attempts to build an entertainment center. Ross is challenged to name the five celebrities he'd like to sleep with most.
